L1.area: Network protocol message handling and session management

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists where certain optional information elements in incoming network protocol messages are not validated before access. When a crafted message omits expected nested fields, the application panics, causing service disruption for all connected subscribers.

L3.area: NGAP protocol message handlers for UE location reporting and connection management
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in NGAP protocol message processing where certain optional information elements such as area-of-interest lists and reporting reference IDs are not checked for nil before being accessed. An attacker able to send crafted NGAP messages can trigger a panic, crashing the process and disrupting service for all connected subscribers.

ground_truth.reason: The NGAP message handlers do not check for nil on optional information elements before accessing their fields, causing a nil pointer dereference panic when processing crafted messages with missing IEs.
